text:
GYNECOLOGIC ONCOLOGY — POST-OPERATIVE FOLLOW-UP NOTE

ATTENDING: [Gynecologic Oncology Attending]
VISIT TYPE: Post-operative oncology follow-up

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Post-operative oncology follow-up visit, approximately 5 weeks after primary debulking surgery (PDS) for newly diagnosed high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Okonkwo is a 58-year-old postmenopausal West African woman, G3P3, who presents today for her first post-operative oncology visit following PDS performed approximately 5 weeks ago. She was initially evaluated in GynOnc clinic after presenting with abdominal distension and bloating. Staging CT at that time demonstrated bilateral ovarian masses, large volume ascites (3.2L drained at time of surgery), diffuse peritoneal carcinomatosis with omental cake, and pelvic sidewall lymphadenopathy. CA-125 at diagnosis was markedly elevated at 4,820 U/mL. Omental biopsy confirmed high-grade serous carcinoma (HGSOC), PAX8+, WT-1+, p53 aberrant, Ki-67 80%. Germline testing returned positive for a pathogenic BRCA1 variant (c.5266dupC, also known as 5382insC). Fagotti score was 6 at time of pre-operative assessment, and the decision was made to proceed with upfront PDS.

She underwent primary debulking via midline laparotomy including TAH-BSO, omentectomy, pelvic and para-aortic lymph node dissection, peritoneal stripping, rectosigmoid resection with primary anastomosis, appendectomy, cholecystectomy, and splenectomy. Pathologic stage confirmed pT3c pN1 M0 (FIGO IIIC): bilateral ovarian HGSOC with STIC in the right tube, involvement of omentum, diaphragm, peritoneum, rectosigmoid serosa, and splenic hilum. Four of 18 pelvic lymph nodes and 2 of 14 para-aortic lymph nodes were positive. Estimated blood loss was 1,850 mL. R0 resection was achieved.

Post-operative course was complicated by Grade 2 ileus noted on POD4, managed conservatively with NGT decompression and bowel rest, and a Grade 1 wound seroma. She was ultimately discharged on POD12 after return of bowel function and clinical improvement.

Since discharge, she reports overall improving energy levels, though she remains fatigued. She has been tolerating a regular diet without difficulty. No abdominal pain, no nausea or vomiting. Bowel function has normalized; she is having daily soft formed stools without blood. She denies dysuria, urinary incontinence, or urinary retention. She reports mild incisional soreness with activity, which is improving. She denies fever or chills. No lower extremity swelling or calf pain. Appetite is decreased from baseline but improved compared to immediately post-discharge.

Today's visit focused on: (1) wound and functional recovery assessment, (2) review of final surgical pathology and molecular results, (3) adjuvant treatment planning, (4) genetic counseling referral, and (5) discussion of PARP inhibitor maintenance.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Constitutional: Fatigue, moderate. No fever, no night sweats, no significant weight loss beyond surgical period.
Gastrointestinal: Improved appetite, tolerating regular diet, normal BMs, no nausea. Mild residual bloating.
GU: Normal urinary frequency. No hematuria. No pelvic pain.
Neurologic: No numbness, tingling, or burning in hands or feet at baseline.
Musculoskeletal: Mild incisional discomfort with movement.
Cardiopulmonary: No dyspnea, no cough, no chest pain or palpitations.
Dermatologic: Incision healing, mild seroma expressed at last wound check. No erythema.
Psychosocial: Reports anxiety related to cancer diagnosis; discussing with primary care. Supportive family present today.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY
1. Hypertension — managed with lisinopril 10 mg daily
2. Remote appendectomy (prior to current surgery; appendix also removed at time of PDS)
3. High-grade serous ovarian carcinoma, FIGO IIIC, BRCA1-mutated — as above

FAMILY HISTORY
Mother: Breast cancer, diagnosed age 45
Maternal aunt: Ovarian cancer
No known colorectal or pancreatic cancers in family. BRCA1 familial status pending cascade testing.

SOCIAL HISTORY
Non-smoker. Occasional alcohol use, denies current alcohol. Lives with adult daughter. Retired school administrator. West African descent. Postmenopausal.

ALLERGIES
NKDA

MEDICATIONS
1. Lisinopril 10 mg PO daily
2. Ferrous sulfate 325 mg PO daily (started post-operatively for anemia)
3. Docusate sodium 100 mg PO BID
4. Ondansetron 4 mg PO PRN nausea
5. Oxycodone 5 mg PO PRN pain (tapering down, reports minimal use at this point)

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
Vitals: BP 128/76 mmHg, HR 82 bpm, RR 16, Temp 98.4°F, SpO2 98% on room air, Weight 71.2 kg (pre-surgical baseline ~74 kg)
Performance Status: ECOG 1

General: Alert and oriented x3, well-appearing, in no acute distress. Ambulating independently.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Sclerae anicteric. Oropharynx without lesions or mucositis.
Neck: No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs. No lower extremity edema.
Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No dullness to percussion.
Abdomen: Midline laparotomy incision well-approximated, staples removed, Steri-Strips in place. Mild serous residual of seroma at inferior aspect of wound — no erythema, warmth, or purulent drainage. Abdomen soft. Mild tenderness to deep palpation in lower midline. No guarding or rebound. No hepatosplenomegaly palpated (post-splenectomy). Bowel sounds present.
Pelvic: Deferred today given recent surgery (post-op week 5); vaginal cuff intact per report of discharging surgical team, no examination performed today.
Extremities: No calf tenderness. No DVT signs.
Neurologic: Cranial nerves grossly intact. Gait normal. No observed sensory deficits in extremities. Baseline neuropathy assessment: none.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS
Drawn today (~4 weeks from surgery):
- WBC: 7.2 K/uL
- Hgb: 9.8 g/dL (mild normocytic anemia; down from pre-surgical 12.1, consistent with surgical blood loss and post-operative recovery)
- Plt: 312 K/uL
- MCV: 88 fL
- Creatinine: 0.82 mg/dL
- BUN: 14 mg/dL
- AST: 28 U/L
- ALT: 31 U/L
- ALP: 88 U/L
- Total Bilirubin: 0.6 mg/dL
- Albumin: 3.3 g/dL (low-normal; nutritional impact of surgery expected)
- CA-125: 847 U/mL (down markedly from 4,820 U/mL at diagnosis; expected post-operative nadir trajectory ongoing)

Note: Hgb of 9.8 monitored, patient is asymptomatic at rest; will continue iron supplementation. Transfusion not required at this time.

---

IMAGING
No new imaging obtained today. Will plan post-cycle 3 restaging CT as per standard protocol.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. HIGH-GRADE SEROUS OVARIAN CARCINOMA, FIGO IIIC, pT3c pN1 M0, BRCA1 germline mutated, R0 resection achieved
   - Patient is approximately 5 weeks post PDS with excellent functional recovery. ECOG 1. Wound healing appropriately. R0 resection achieved — excellent surgical outcome given disease burden at presentation.
   - CA-125 trending down significantly (4,820 → 847 U/mL), consistent with favorable response to debulking. Will continue to monitor as chemotherapy begins.
   - Plan to initiate adjuvant chemotherapy: Paraplatin AUC 5 / Taxol 175 mg/m2 IV every 21 days x 6 cycles (standard GOG-158 backbone). Target start within the next 1-2 weeks once we confirm adequate counts and functional status.

2. Avastin (Avastin) — DEFERRED
   - Discussed the option of adding bev (Avastin) to the backbone regimen per GOG-0218 data, with potential benefit in high-risk stage III/IV disease. However, given the recent rectosigmoid resection with anastomosis and concern for impaired wound healing and anastomotic integrity, Avastin initiation is deferred. Patient also expressed personal preference against it at this time given these concerns. Will revisit in the maintenance setting if clinically appropriate, though PARP inhibitor is the priority maintenance strategy given BRCA1 status.

3. ADJUVANT CHEMOTHERAPY PRE-TREATMENT WORKUP
   - Baseline labs reviewed and acceptable for chemotherapy start (ANC adequate, Plt 312, Cr 0.82).
   - Mild anemia (Hgb 9.8) — not a contraindication to initiation; will continue iron. Repeat CBC prior to C1 Day 1.
   - Baseline neuropathy: none. Counseled patient on cumulative pacli-related peripheral neuropathy; will assess at each cycle.
   - Antiemetic prophylaxis plan: ondansetron + dexamethasone + fosaprepitant per Paraplatin/Taxol protocol.
   - Compazine PRN breakthrough.

4. BRCA1 GERMLINE RESULT — REFERRAL TO GENETIC COUNSELING
   - Reviewed germline BRCA1 c.5266dupC (pathogenic) result in detail with patient today. Counseled regarding implications for personal cancer surveillance and risk in family members.
   - Formal referral placed to genetic counseling for cascade testing of her three adult children. Patient expressed appropriate understanding and agreed to share results with family.
   - Discussed BRCA1-related increased risk for breast cancer and other BRCA-associated malignancies; breast surveillance to be coordinated with PCP/breast imaging.

5. PARP INHIBITOR MAINTENANCE — PLAN DISCUSSED
   - Following completion of 6 cycles of platinum-taxane chemotherapy, patient is an excellent candidate for PARP inhibitor maintenance given BRCA1 germline mutation. Options discussed include nira (Zejula) and Lynparza (Lynparza); both FDA-approved in this setting.
   - Will finalize choice of agent and dosing at the post-C6 visit based on response, counts, and tolerability during chemotherapy.
   - Patient is engaged and understands the rationale. Counseled regarding common toxicities (thrombocytopenia, anemia, nausea, fatigue for Zejula; similar plus potential for MDS/AML long-term for both).

6. ANEMIA
   - Continue ferrous sulfate 325 mg daily. Recheck CBC at C1 Day 1. If Hgb declines further or symptoms develop, will consider transfusion or erythropoiesis-stimulating agent per guidelines.

7. PSYCHOSOCIAL
   - Patient reports anxiety related to new diagnosis; discussed normalcy of this and importance of support resources. Referral to oncology social work placed. Patient has strong family support.

8. FOLLOW-UP
   - C1 Day 1: Scheduled in approximately 10 days pending pre-chemo labs. Infusion appointment placed.
   - Follow-up in clinic after C1 to assess tolerability.
   - Restaging CT and CA-125 planned after C3.
   - Genetic counseling appointment pending scheduling.

Time spent: 55 minutes (>50% counseling and coordination of care).

Electronically signed,
[Gynecologic Oncology Attending Physician]
